title: Adaptive forwarding handling of data packets
abstract: The invention refers to a method being performed by a wireless device, UE, to forward data of an application patent unit, PDU, the application PDU being generated by an application in the UE, wherein this wherein the UE performs the steps of: segmenting (100) the application PDU into a plurality of data packets, determining (101) one or a plurality of Quality-of-Service, QoS, parameters associated to the application PDU, determining (102) that different data packets need a different forwarding treatment to comply with the one or the plurality of QoS parameters, and mapping (103) the different data packets to different data radio bearers, DRBs, or providing a different treatment for forwarding in one DRB; the invention further refers to corresponding methods in a code network and in a radio access, RAN node; the invention further relates to a corresponding UE, a core network node and a RAN node.
claims: Claims 1. A method performed by a wireless device, UE (10), to forward data of an application packed data unit, PDU, wherein the UE performs the steps of: â¢ segmenting (100) the application PDU into a plurality of data packets, â¢ determining (101) one or a plurality of Quality-of-Service, QoS, parameters associated to the application PDU, â¢ determining (102) that different data packets need a different forwarding treatment to comply with the one or the plurality of QoS parameters, and â¢ mapping (103) the different data packets to different data radio bearers, DRBs. 2. The method of Claim 1, wherein the plurality of data packets are mapped to a selected QoS flow out of a plurality of QoS flows, wherein each QoS flow is characterized by a corresponding QoS profile. 3. The method of Claim 2, wherein as result of determining that different data packets within the QoS flow need a different forwarding treatment, assigning different priority identifiers to the different data packets, and wherein the mapping of the different data packets to the different DRBs is performed based on the priority identifiers. 4. The method of Claim 3, wherein the priority identifiers are QoS flow identifiers, QFIs, or wherein the priority identifiers each result from a combination of a QFI and one or more further priority values. 5. The method of Claims 2-4, further comprising configuring a plurality of QoS flows associated each to an appropriate QoS profile of a plurality of QoS profiles and mapping the different data packets to different QoS flows of the plurality of QoS flows. 6. The method of Claims 1- 5, wherein the priority identifiers are associated to an urgency to deliver a packet to one or more of the following: a radio access network, RAN, node, a node in the core network, or a server connected to the core network. 7. The method of Claim 1-6, wherein the UE is provided with or determines a maximum application PDU delay for delivering the application PDU to over the RAN node to a recipient application function. 8. The method of Claim 7, wherein, based on the maximum application PDU delay, the UE determines for each of the different data packets of the application PDU an individual packet delay budget and performs mapping the different data packets to different data radio bearers, DRBs based on one of: the corresponding individual packet delay budget, and a combination of the corresponding individual packet delay budget and further treatment information, such as a reliability requirement and a packet size of the individual data packet. 9. The method of the preceding Claim, wherein if the individual packet delay budget, or a value indicative of the combination of the individual packet delay budget and the further treatment information of a data packet is above a certain threshold, marking this data packet with a first priority identifier and/or mapping it to a first DRB, and otherwise marking this data packet with a second QFI and/or mapping it to a second DRB. 10. The method of the preceding Claim, wherein the marking and/or mapping is performed by a service data adaptation protocol, SDAP, function within the UE. 11. The method of Claims 2-10, further comprising receiving from a core network node a plurality of QFIs to be applied to the data packets and rules for how to use each QFI for the plurality of data packets. 12. The method of Claim 11, wherein the rules are provided by a set of Non-Stratum Access, NAS, filters and/or a set of Access Stratum filters to be applied by the UE. 13. The method of Claims 11-12, wherein the Rules comprise QoS rules, NAS filters, Packet Detection Rules, PDRs. 14. The method of any of the preceding Claims, wherein the data packet is an internet protocol, IP packet. 15. A wireless device, UE (10), the UE comprising: â¢ radio interface circuitry (11) configured to communicate with a network node via at least one cell; and â¢ processing circuitry (12) operably coupled to the radio interface circuitry; â¢ whereby the processing circuitry (12) and the radio interface circuitry (11) are configured to perform the steps of Claims 1-14. 16. A method performed by a User Plane Function, UPF (30), of a core network, wherein the UPF receives an application packed data unit, PDU, from an application function of another device or node, wherein the UPF performs the steps of: â¢ segmenting (300) the application PDU into a plurality of data packets, â¢ determining (301) one or a plurality of Quality-of-Service, QoS, parameters associated to the application PDU, â¢ determining (302) that different data packets need a different forwarding treatment (or shall be differently treated) to comply with the one or the plurality of QoS parameters, â¢ transmitting (303) the plurality of data packets to a radio access network, RAN, node for transmitting the data packets to the UE, and â¢ thereby indicating (304) a need for the different forwarding treatment of the different data packets to the RAN node. 17. The method of Claim 16, wherein the plurality of data packets are to be mapped to a selected QoS flow out of a plurality of QoS flows, wherein each QoS flow is characterized by a corresponding QoS profile. 18. The method of Claim 16-17 , wherein indicating the need for the different forwarding treatment to the RAN node comprises assigning different priority identifiers to different data packets, to be used by the RAN node to map each data packet to a corresponding QoS flow and/or to map it to a corresponding DRB. 19. The method of Claim 18, wherein the priority identifiers are QoS flow identifiers, QFIs, or wherein the propriety identifiers each result from a combination of a QFI and one or more further priority values. 20. The method of Claims 17-19, further comprising sending configuration information to the RAN node for configuring a plurality of QoS flows associated each to an appropriate QoS profile of a plurality of QoS profiles. 21. The method of Claim 20, further transmitting to the RAN node information indicative of mapping rules for mapping the different data packets to different DRBs of a plurality of DRBs and/or information for adapting a priority identifier associated to a data packet. 22. The method of Claims 16-21, wherein the one or a plurality of Quality-of-Service, QoS, parameters comprise an information about an urgency to deliver a data packet to a recipient application function of another device or node. 23. The method of Claim 22, wherein the information comprises a maximum application delay for transmitting the application PDU to the recipient application function. 24. A User Plane Function, UPF (30), of a core network node comprising: â¢ an interface circuitry (31) configured to communicate with a radio access network, RAN, node; and â¢ processing circuitry (32) operably coupled to the interface circuitry, â¢ whereby the processing circuitry and the interface circuitry are configured to perform any of the steps of Claims 16-23. 25. A non-transitory, computer-readable medium storing computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry of a UE, configure the UE to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of Claims 1-14. 26. A computer program product comprising computer-executable instructions that, when executed by processing circuitry of a wireless device, configure the wireless device to perform operations corresponding to any of the methods of Claims 1-14. 27. A method, performed by a Radio Access Network, RAN, node (20) to forward data of an application packed data unit, PDU, wherein the RAN node performs the steps of: â¢ receiving (200) a plurality of data packets associated to an application PDU from a User Plane Function, UPF, of a core network, â¢ receiving (201) an indicating from the UPF about (a need for) a different forwarding treatment of the different data packets to a wireless device, UE, â¢ mapping (202) the different data packets to different data radio bearers, DRBs according the (need for a) different forwarding treatment, and â¢ transmitting (203) the data packets. 28. The method of Claim 27, wherein the plurality of data packets are mapped to a selected QoS flow out of a plurality of QoS flows, wherein each QoS flow is characterized by a corresponding QoS profile. 29. The method of Claim 27-28, wherein the indication about the need for the different forwarding treatment comprises an assignation of different priority identifiers to the different data packets and mapping each data packet to a corresponding QoS flow and/or a to a corresponding DRB. 30. The method of Claims 27-28, further comprising configuring a plurality of QoS flows associated each to an appropriate QoS profile of a plurality of QoS profiles. 31. The method of Claim 30, further receiving from a User Plane Function, UPF information indicative of mapping rules for mapping the different IP data packets to different DRBs of a plurality of DRBs and/or information for adapting a priority identifier associated to a data packet. 32. The method of Claims 27-31, wherein the one or a plurality of Quality-of-Service, QoS, parameters comprise an information about an urgency to deliver a data packet to another a recipient application function of another device or node. 33. The method of Claim 32, wherein the information comprises a maximum application delay for transmitting the application PDU to the other recipient application function. 34. The method of Claim 33, wherein, based on the maximum application PDU delay, the RAN node determines for each of the different data packets of the application PDU an individual packet delay budget and performs mapping the different data packets to different data radio bearers, DRBs based on one of: the corresponding individual packet delay budget, and a combination of the corresponding individual packet delay budget and further treatment information, such as a reliability requirement and a packet size of the individual data packet. 35. The method of the preceding Claim, wherein if the individual packet delay budget, or a value indicative of the combination of the individual packet delay budget and the further treatment information of a data packet is above a certain threshold, marking this data packet with a first priority identifier and/or mapping it to a first DRB, and otherwise marking this data packet with a second QFI and/or mapping it to a second DRB. 36. The method of the preceding Claim, wherein the marking and/or mapping is performed by a service data adaptation protocol, SDAP, function within the RAN node. 37. A Radio Access Network, RAN, node (20), the RAN node comprising: â¢ radio interface circuitry (21) configured to communicate with a UE; â¢ an interface circuitry configured to communicate with a UPF of a core network; â¢ processing circuitry (92) operably coupled to interface circuitries; â¢ whereby the processing circuitry and the interface circuitries being configured to perform any of the steps of Claims 27-36.
